title: Missing values during insert
summary: Learn how SQL handles missing values during INSERT, including the use of NULL and DEFAULT values in the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: Handling Missing Values in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data into a table like `inventory_items`, you don't always have to provide a value for every column. If a column allows NULL or has a DEFAULT value, you can omit it in your INSERT statement. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Desk Mat', 'Accessories', 19.99);
```

Here, columns like `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` will use their DEFAULT values, and `notes` will be set to NULL (since it allows NULLs).

sketchBlocks.title: Explicit NULL vs. Omitted Columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can explicitly insert NULL into a column that allows it:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes) VALUES ('Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 3.50, NULL);
```

Or, you can simply omit the column from your column list, and SQL will use the DEFAULT value (if defined) or NULL (if allowed).

sketchBlocks.title: What Happens If You Omit a NOT NULL Column?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: If you try to insert a row without providing a value for a NOT NULL column that has no DEFAULT, SQL will return an error. Always check your table schema to know which columns require values and which can be omitted.

quizDraft.title: Insert with omitted columns using defaults
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item named 'Gift Card' in the 'Accessories' category with a price of 25.00. Only provide values for name, category, and price. The other columns should use their default values or NULL where allowed.
quizDraft.hint: List only the columns you want to provide. The rest will use DEFAULT or NULL if possible.

help.concept: When you omit columns in an INSERT statement, SQL fills in missing values with the column's DEFAULT or NULL (if allowed).
help.hint_1: Only specify name, category, and price in your column list.
help.hint_2: Do not include columns like status, is_test, notes, or last_updated in your INSERT statement.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Gift Card', 'Accessories', 25.00);

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit NULL
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item named 'Sample Notebook' in the 'Accessories' category with a price of 8.00. Set the notes column to NULL explicitly. Use only the required columns and notes.
quizDraft.hint: Include the notes column and set its value to NULL.

help.concept: You can explicitly insert NULL into columns that allow it by listing the column and using NULL as the value.
help.hint_1: Include notes in your column list and use NULL as its value.
help.hint_2: Your INSERT should specify name, category, price, and notes.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes) VALUES ('Sample Notebook', 'Accessories', 8.00, NULL);

quizDraft.title: Insert with all required columns, omitting optional
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item named 'Trial Keychain' in the 'Accessories' category with a price of 2.50. Do not provide a value for notes. Only include the required columns.
quizDraft.hint: Omit the notes column; it allows NULL.

help.concept: If a column allows NULL and you omit it in your INSERT, SQL will set it to NULL.
help.hint_1: Only list name, category, and price in your INSERT.
help.hint_2: Do not mention notes in your column list.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Trial Keychain', 'Accessories', 2.50);

quizDraft.title: Which columns can be omitted in an INSERT?
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following columns in the inventory_items table can be omitted from an INSERT statement and still allow the row to be inserted successfully? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns that have a DEFAULT value or allow NULL.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT value or that allow NULL can be omitted from an INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Check the schema for columns with DEFAULT or NULL allowed.
help.hint_2: Omit columns like notes, status, is_test, and last_updated if they have defaults or allow NULL.

quizDraft.title: Default value for status
quizDraft.prompt: What value will the status column have if you omit it in an INSERT statement for inventory_items?
quizDraft.hint: Check the DEFAULT value for status in the table schema.

help.concept: If a column has a DEFAULT value and is omitted in an INSERT, SQL uses the DEFAULT.
help.hint_1: Look at the CREATE TABLE statement for the default value of status.
help.hint_2: The default for status is a string set in the schema.

quizDraft.template: If you omit the status column in an INSERT, its value will be: ___
